skill.name: maxhub-twitter
skill.description: Twitter/X 数据查询助手。覆盖推文详情、用户资料、搜索、评论、趋势等全功能。
skill.summary: Twitter/X Data Assistant for querying tweets, user profiles, search, comments, and trending topics via the MaxHub API.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: All 171 static scan findings are false positives. This skill is a prompt-based SKILL.md file that instructs AI assistants on how to query the MaxHub Twitter/X API. External command patterns (92 instances) are markdown code block examples showing curl usage, not executable code. Environment variable access (16 instances) is the legitimate MAXHUB_API_KEY required for API authentication. Weak cryptographic algorithm matches (24 instances) are false positives triggered by numeric tweet IDs in documentation. Network URL references point exclusively to the legitimate MaxHub API service at aconfig.cn. No prompt injection, obfuscation, or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: External command patterns in documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected 92 instances of shell backtick execution patterns. All instances are curl commands inside markdown code blocks in documentation files, not executable scripts. These are example API calls for the AI to reference. No command injection risk exists.

low_findings.title: Environment variable access for API authentication
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 16 instances of API key environment variable access. All reference MAXHUB_API_KEY which is the documented authentication credential for the MaxHub API. This is standard and expected behavior for an API client skill. The skill properly declares the key as required and sensitive.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs pointing to API service
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 28 instances of hardcoded URLs. All URLs reference the legitimate MaxHub API service at https://www.aconfig.cn or social media platforms twitter.com/x.com for documentation examples. No malicious endpoints detected.

content.user_title: Query Twitter/X data with AI-powered API assistant
content.value_statement: Marketing teams and researchers struggle to extract structured Twitter/X data for analysis. This skill connects AI assistants to the MaxHub API, enabling natural language queries for tweet details, user profiles, search results, and trending topics.
actual_capabilities: Fetch tweet details and comments by tweet ID
Search tweets by keyword with Top, Latest, Media, People, and Lists result types
Retrieve user profiles, posts, media, followers, and followings
Get trending topics filtered by country
Fetch retweet user lists and user tweet replies
Support both Chinese and English language queries with localized number formatting
limitations: Requires a valid MaxHub API key to function — no built-in free tier
Data accuracy and availability depend on the third-party MaxHub API service
Cannot post, delete, or modify tweets — read-only queries only
Rate limits apply based on the user's MaxHub API plan

use_cases.title: Social media sentiment monitoring
use_cases.description: Track brand mentions and public sentiment on Twitter/X in real time. Search for keywords related to your brand, analyze engagement metrics, and monitor conversation volume over time.

use_cases.title: Trending topics research
use_cases.description: Discover and analyze trending topics across different countries. Identify emerging conversations, track discussion patterns, and understand regional differences in public discourse.

use_cases.title: KOL influence assessment
use_cases.description: Evaluate influencer profiles by examining follower counts, engagement rates, and content themes. Compare multiple creators side by side to inform campaign partnership decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Look up a tweet by ID
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the tweet with ID 1808168603721650364 and show me the author, text, like count, and retweet count.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick lookup of a single tweet's details and metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Search for recent tweets by keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for the latest tweets about artificial intelligence and summarize the top 5 results with engagement metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Twitter user profile
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the profile of user elonmusk and fetch their recent 10 posts. Provide an analysis of posting frequency, content themes, and average engagement.
prompt_templates.scenario: In-depth user analysis combining profile data and post history.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-endpoint deep dive report
prompt_templates.prompt: Do a full analysis of user elonmusk: get their profile, recent posts, followers, followings, and media. Compare engagement across post types and provide a comprehensive report.
prompt_templates.scenario: Deep research combining multiple API endpoints for a complete user analysis.

output_examples.input: Show me the trending topics in Japan right now
output: Current trending topics in Japan (as of query time):
1. #AIArtContest — 125K posts — New AI art competition generates discussion
2. 新幹線 — 89K posts — High-speed rail service updates
4. 気象情報 — 54K posts — Weather alerts and forecasts
Key insights: Technology and entertainment dominate current Japanese trends.

output_examples.input: Find the latest comments on tweet 1808168603721650364 and summarize the sentiment
output: Latest comments on the tweet (25 comments found, showing top 10):
Positive (6 comments): Supportive messages praising the announcement
Neutral (3 comments): Factual questions and clarifications
Negative (1 comment): Criticism of the proposed timeline
Overall sentiment is leaning positive (60%). Most users express excitement.

best_practices: Always check if MAXHUB_API_KEY is configured before making API calls. If missing, guide the user through the setup process.
Use parallel API calls for independent queries to reduce response time and improve user experience.
Format numbers according to the user's detected language: use K/M/B for English and wan/yi for Chinese.
anti_patterns: Do not display raw API credentials or API keys in any output. Keep all authentication details internal.
Do not assume the API key is configured. Always verify before attempting requests and provide setup guidance if missing.
Do not make assumptions about data without querying the API. Base all conclusions on actual API responses and report empty results honestly.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need a valid MaxHub API key from aconfig.cn. The skill checks for this key on every request.

faq.question: Can this skill post tweets or direct messages?
faq.answer: No. This skill is read-only. It can only fetch tweet details, user profiles, search results, and trending data.

faq.question: What languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports Chinese and English. It detects the user's language and responds in the same language.

faq.question: What happens if the API key is invalid or missing?
faq.answer: The skill shows a setup guide that walks the user through registering, creating an API key, and configuring the environment variable.

faq.question: What Twitter/X data can I access?
faq.answer: You can access tweet details, comments, retweet lists, user profiles, user posts, user media, followers, followings, search results, and trending topics by country.

faq.question: Is there pagination support for large result sets?
faq.answer: Yes. Most endpoints support cursor-based pagination. You can request the next page of results using the cursor from the previous response.
